programme.desc: As Buck's life hangs in the balance, he dreams of a world where he never became a firefighter, for better and worse.

programme.desc: Despite the case being closed, Bobby is determined to prove his Alcoholics Anonymous sponsor was murdered and bring his killer to justice; Buck struggles with his post-traumatic stress.

programme.desc: The 118 faces emergencies at a spin class and a hair salon; a husband and wife find themselves in a compromising position; Buck discovers new cognitive abilities; Hen and Karen find out Denny has been seeing his biological father.

programme.desc: After a female pundit is assaulted during a protest, Benson and Barba struggle to put their political beliefs aside to help bring the attacker to justice.

programme.title: Live: Premier League Soccer
programme.desc: Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur meet at Villa Park. 5th-place Villa looks to jump into the top four with a 17th victory of the term, while 18th-place Spurs desperately need points to escape the drop zone. Villa won the reverse fixture 2-1.

programme.desc: The best moments from the weekend in Premier League action.

programme.desc: Braun storms Fort Worth for ribs, shareables, and a deep dive into Texas food and heritage.

programme.desc: The death of a young girl appears to link to the world of ultimate fighting, and tensions rise within the department when two brothers (Gaius Charles, Arlen Escarpeta) are charged with the crime.

programme.desc: When a janitor violently attacks a coach, the SVU team connects the janitor's behavior to a history of sexual abuse; the detectives find more victims of the same predator -- including a prisoner (Mike Tyson) on death row.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When a teenager is found dead in the Hudson River, Lt. Benson suspects Rob Miller is behind the mysterious death but can't prove it; despite having only circumstantial evidence, ADA Stone takes the case to court before Miller can destroy more lives.

programme.title: Live: Premier League Mornings
programme.desc: Stateside Premier League fan festival that includes a turf pitch, the Premier League trophy, club mascots, special guests, contests, and more.

programme.title: Live: Premier League Soccer
programme.desc: Chelsea (13-9-12) returns to Stamford Bridge as they face Nottingham Forest (10-9-5). Chelsea looks to rebound from a 3-0 loss to Brighton after recently sacking head coach Liam Rosenior. The Tricky Trees have officially avoided the relegation zone.

programme.title: Live: Premier League Goal Zone
programme.desc: The best moments from the weekend in Premier League action.

programme.title: Everything on the Menu With Braun Strowman
programme.desc: In Vegas, Braun bets it all on flavor and finds the city's true magic to be an irresistible bite.

programme.title: Everything on the Menu With Braun Strowman
programme.desc: After Chicago chaos in the ring, Braun swaps slams for dogs, edible balloons and astonishing bites.

programme.title: Everything on the Menu With Braun Strowman
programme.desc: In New Orleans, Braun devours bold flavors that hit harder than a steel chair.

programme.desc: Discussion and analysis of Premier League soccer.

programme.title: Live: Premier League Soccer
programme.desc: Manchester City (21-7-5) visits Everton (13-8-13) at Hill Dickinson Stadium. Man City trail Arsenal in a tight title race. For Everton, Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall and Beto have each scored eight goals this season as Toffees look to defend their home turf.

programme.title: Live: Premier League Goal Zone
programme.desc: The best moments from the weekend in Premier League action.

programme.title: Everything on the Menu With Braun Strowman
programme.desc: Braun vacations in Tampa, where the food might tap out before he does.

programme.desc: Rollins' sister stirs up trouble and a possible SVU investigation when she reports a doctor who trades prescription painkillers for sex.
